title: Nail psoriasis review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting psoriasis type, duration, extent, distribution, nails, high-impact sites, symptoms, systemic upset, daily-life impact, and wellbeing without inferring an individual diagnosis or treatment.
assessment.rationale: The exact psoriasis assessment section explicitly includes nail involvement and supports documenting duration, interval change, symptoms, functional or quality-of-life impact, distribution and clinician-observed nail or skin findings without inferring diagnosis or treatment.

source.title: Psoriasis: assessment and management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with psoriasis, including skin, nail, joint, severity, impact, and referral qualifiers.
source.setting: Primary and specialist assessment and management of psoriasis.
source.exclusions: Exact for psoriasis identity, distribution, extent, nail involvement, high-impact sites, symptom and life impact, systemic upset, and joint-symptom assessment. Treatment and referral decisions are not mapped, and UAE adaptation remains required.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1.1–1.2.1.12 — assessment, severity, sites, and impact

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting psoriasis type, duration, extent, distribution, nails, high-impact sites, symptoms, systemic upset, daily-life impact, and wellbeing without inferring an individual diagnosis or treatment.

assessment.text: Supports asking about joint symptoms and recording clinician-led specialist assessment or referral only when completed or arranged.
assessment.rationale: Joint-symptom and clinician-led referral qualifiers were reviewed, but no arthritis diagnosis or referral is generated.
